# rightProjectionMatrix

**Instance Property**

The transformation matrix that determines the extent of a scene visible to the camera’s right viewpoint.

## Declaration

```swift
var rightProjectionMatrix: matrix_float4x4 { get }
```

## Discussion

Model I/O  automatically generates this matrix from the same properties that produce the [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLCamera/projectionMatrix] property of a monocular camera, additionally using the [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLStereoscopicCamera/interPupillaryDistance] and [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLStereoscopicCamera/rightVergence] properties to account for the offset and angle of a stereoscopic camera’s left viewpoint.

A renderer uses this matrix, along with the [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLStereoscopicCamera/rightViewMatrix] property and model matrices derived from the camera’s position and orientation (the [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLObject/transform] property) and the content to be rendered, to transform vertex data to the renderer’s 2D screen space at render time.

## Generating View and Projection Matrices

- **leftViewMatrix**: The transformation matrix that determines the position and orientation of the camera’s left viewpoint relative to a scene.
- **leftProjectionMatrix**: The transformation matrix that determines the extent of a scene visible to the camera’s left viewpoint.
- **rightViewMatrix**: The transformation matrix that determines the position and orientation of the camera’s right viewpoint relative to a scene.
